_description: Model derivations for Mexico vs England 2026-07-05/06 R16

rule_checks.RULE7: INACTIVE -- Mexico win=31.01% well above the 15% threshold, a genuinely close match
rule_checks.RULE8: FIRES HARD -- Draw=31.01%, tied with Mexico's own win probability, the highest draw signal seen in a while
rule_checks.WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS_APPLIED: Q10 (England 5+ SOT) is the SAME team and SAME question type that already lost -11.36 RBP once this campaign (ENG-CDR '7+ SOT') from blending toward empirical. Market-fit (0.387) vs empirical (0.80-0.90, inflated by excluding/including the Ghana outlier either way) -- staying at market (0.40) as a deliberate non-repeat of an already-realized loss on this exact team/question type.
rule_checks.GHA_ANALOGY_CAUTION: Applied to Q2 (Bellingham 2+ SOT) in the OPPOSITE direction from the historical mistake: rather than suppressing him toward the Ghana-game blank, bumped him toward the average of all 4 games (2+ SOT hit in 2 of 4, including the toughest defensive test to date, Congo DR R32) -- correcting the named overfit rather than repeating it.

espn_insight.mexico_low_corner_trait: 1.67 corners/game across groups -- a durable structural trait, but the market has already burned us once (MEX-ECU, -11.80) by trusting a market that undershot this; approaching Q for corners (Most_corners England 0.60) as a DIRECT market read rather than trying to double down on Mexico's own history again
espn_insight.england_corners_reliable: 8, 9, 7 across all 3 group games -- the most stable England trait, supports trusting the Most_corners market directly
espn_insight.quinones_floor: 1+ SOT in literally every game this tournament (4/4) -- a personal floor (Cluster-A-adjacent logic), much safer than a team-level ceiling projection since the bar is low and he's never missed it
espn_insight.jimenez_scoring_rate: 2 goals in 3 appearances when featured (67%) -- reasonably close to market already
